title: When Things Go Wrong 🔥
description: Don't let your app crash! Learn to anticipate and announce errors.

slides.title: The Reality of Code 🌧️
slides.content:
# Anticipating Failure

No matter how perfect your code is, things will go wrong in the real world. A user will lose Wi-Fi. A server will go down. A file will go missing.

If we let Swift try to load a missing file, the app will instantly crash. To prevent this, we must build a system to gracefully detect and handle these inevitable **Errors**.

slides.title: Defining the Errors 📝
slides.content:
# Using Enums

The very first step in Error Handling is simply creating a list of all the possible things that *could* go wrong in a specific situation.

We do this by creating an **Enum** that adopts the built-in `Error` protocol.

```swift
// A list of things that can go wrong during login
enum LoginError: Error {
    case wrongPassword
    case userNotFound
    case networkOffline
}
```

quizData.question: What must an Enum adopt in order for Swift to formally recognize its cases as throw-able Errors?
quizData.explanation: If your enum does not adopt `Error` (e.g. `enum MyError: Error`), Swift will not allow you to actually 'throw' these cases when something goes wrong.
